result:
The article labels the section "Techniques" (not "Methods") — that is the equivalent section. Here is the JSON result:

```json
{
  "id": "D-15",
  "version": "v1",
  "tool_used": "webfetch",
  "commands_run": ["npx tsx src/cli.ts https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_scraping"],
  "flags_used": [],
  "files_written": ["en.wikipedia.org/wiki-web-scraping-13cfc9.md"],
  "workflow": "fetch-then-read",
  "tool_calls_total": 5,
  "webfetch_calls": 1,
  "read_calls": 3,
  "other_calls": 1,
  "answer_provided": true,
  "notes": "Skill doc says Wikipedia is fine for built-in WebFetch, but task rules required using the CLI. File was ~9634 tokens (>8000), so used Grep to find section headings first, then Read with offset/limit to target the Techniques section (lines 21-50). The Wikipedia article labels the section 'Techniques' not 'Methods' — it covers: Human copy-and-paste (manual), HTTP programming (socket-level requests), HTML parsing (wrapper/template-based extraction), DOM parsing (browser automation via Selenium/Playwright), and Vertical aggregation (automated bots for specific verticals, with ML/CV approaches)."
}
```
